titulo: A primeira semana trabalhando em Berlim
titulo_en: My First Week Working in Berlin
topico: programacao e morar fora do Brasil
resumo_pt: Uma desenvolvedora brasileira conta a primeira semana no novo emprego em Berlim e descobre que o inglês técnico era a parte fácil. O difícil foram as conversas no almoço e o jeito direto de um colega dar feedback.
transcript: So, I moved to Berlin in March for a developer job, and I had spent months getting ready for it. I mean, I watched conference talks, I practised explaining my code out loud, I even wrote little scripts about system design. Technical English, right? That was the thing I was worried about. And it turns out that was the easy part. Nobody cares if you say the wrong word for a database. They understand you anyway. The hard part was lunch. On my second day, six of us went to a place near the office, and everyone started talking at the same time. I understood every single word, and I still had no idea what was funny. Someone would say something about a train, and the whole table would laugh, and I would sit there smiling like a photograph. I could not keep up with it at all. And then there was Jonas, one of the engineers on my team. He looked at my first pull request and he said, this is not good, you should do it again. Just like that. Straight to the point. In Brazil, someone would say, oh, it is really interesting, maybe we could think about another approach. So I went home and I felt terrible. I thought I was going to be fired in my first week. But two days later I noticed he talked to everyone that way, even his manager, and when my second version worked he told me it was excellent, in exactly the same flat voice. Nothing personal. That was just how he spoke. I am still getting the hang of the lunch conversations, honestly. But I stopped taking things personally, and that changed everything.

glossario.significado_pt: acontece que, no fim das contas
glossario.exemplo: And it turns out that was the easy part.
I could not keep up with it at all.

glossario.significado_pt: pedido de revisão de código enviado ao time
glossario.exemplo: He looked at my first pull request and he said, this is not good, you should do it again.

glossario.significado_pt: voz sem emoção, sem variação de tom
glossario.exemplo: when my second version worked he told me it was excellent, in exactly the same flat voice
I am still getting the hang of the lunch conversations, honestly.

glossario.significado_pt: levar as coisas para o lado pessoal
glossario.exemplo: But I stopped taking things personally, and that changed everything.

perguntas.pergunta: What surprised the speaker most about her first week?
opcoes: The technical work was much harder than expected
Her English was too weak for the job
The social side was harder than the technical English
The city was more expensive than she had imagined
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela diz que passou meses se preparando para o inglês técnico, mas que essa acabou sendo a parte fácil. O difícil foi o almoço e as conversas com os colegas.

perguntas.pergunta: How does the speaker feel about her experience by the end?
opcoes: She regrets moving to Berlin
She has adapted completely and has no difficulties
She is angry with her colleague Jonas
She still struggles with some things but has made progress
perguntas.explicacao_pt: No fim ela admite que ainda está pegando o jeito das conversas no almoço, mas que parou de levar as coisas para o lado pessoal, e isso mudou tudo.

perguntas.pergunta: Why does the speaker mention how people give feedback in Brazil?
opcoes: To show how different Jonas's style felt to her
To say that Brazilian feedback is more useful
To explain why she decided to leave Brazil
To complain that her Brazilian colleagues were dishonest
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela compara o comentário seco de Jonas com o jeito brasileiro de suavizar a crítica, para mostrar o contraste que a chocou.

perguntas.pergunta: How many people went to lunch with the speaker?
opcoes: Two
Six in total, including her
Six other colleagues plus her
The whole company
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela diz que seis pessoas foram a um lugar perto do escritório, incluindo ela mesma.

perguntas.pergunta: What did Jonas say about her first pull request?
opcoes: That it was interesting but needed another approach
That he would fix it himself
That it was not good and she should do it again
That it was excellent
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Jonas disse exatamente que não estava bom e que ela deveria fazer de novo. A palavra excelente veio depois, sobre a segunda versão.

perguntas.pergunta: How did the speaker prepare before moving?
opcoes: She took a course in German
She watched conference talks and practised explaining her code
She worked remotely for the company for a year
She read books about German culture
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela conta que assistiu a palestras de conferências, praticou explicar o próprio código em voz alta e escreveu textos curtos sobre design de sistemas.

perguntas.pergunta: Why did the speaker stop worrying about Jonas's comment?
opcoes: Because her manager told her to ignore him
Because Jonas apologised for being rude
Because she realised he spoke that way to everyone, regardless of quality
Because she decided to avoid working with him
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela percebeu dois dias depois que ele falava assim com todo mundo, até com o gerente, e que elogiou a segunda versão com a mesma voz sem emoção. Ela conclui que não era nada pessoal, apenas o jeito dele.
